channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: As campaign nears finish, Walz adds dashes of digs to his portions of joy
item.description: The Democratic vice presidential candidate entered the race emphasizing positivity in politics. In the final stretch to Election Day, he’s trying to thread the needle between needling Donald Trump and JD Vance while still keeping an optimistic message.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: As Biden meets with European allies, the possibility of another Trump term hangs over their talks
item.description: President Joe Biden is in Berlin for a day of closed-door meetings with European allies about Russia’s war in Ukraine and the conflict in the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Josh Boak, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Walz says 90-year-old mom lives off social security check during North Carolina rally
item.description: During his first of two campaign stops in North Carolina, Minnesota Governor Tim Walz claimed that his mother has to depend on her social security checks each month to survive.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Republicans appeal a Georgia judge’s ruling that invalidates seven election rules
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — National and state Republicans on Thursday appealed a judge's ruling that said seven election rules recently passed by Georgia's State Election Board are “illegal, unconstitutional and void.
item.dc:creator: KATE BRUMBACK and JEFF AMY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Democrats in tight House races ride Harris fundraising momentum to outpace GOP
item.description: Democratic candidates in the most competitive House races overwhelmingly outraised their Republican opponents between July and September, buoyed by a surge in enthusiasm when Vice President Harris replaced President Biden as the party’s nominee.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Giorno, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 3 states renew efforts to limit abortion pill access
item.description: The lawsuit was filed in a Texas federal court Friday, roughly four months after the Supreme Court decided against undoing changes to the FDA’s regulations on the abortion pill that expanded access — such as allowing it to be mailed without an in-person doctor’s visit.
item.dc:creator: Alejandra O'Connell-Domenech, Fox 5

item.title: Biden says Sinwar's death is an 'opportunity' for a hostage deal and an end to war in Gaza
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — President Joe Biden said Thursday that the killing of Hamas leader Yahya Sinwar by Israeli troops is a “good day for the world,” calling it an opportunity to free hostages held by the militant group and end the yearlong war in Gaza. “Now’s the time to move on.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK and ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Almost 23% of people say politics influences where they live: Poll
item.description: "Some go so far as to consider moving to find that alignment," Danielle Hale, chief economist for Realtor.com, said.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, NewsNation

item.title: Key Pennsylvania region sees ‘waitlist’ for Trump signs; lawmaker says voters ready to reverse Dem policies
item.description: Lehigh County Republican State Sen. Jarrett Coleman and Northampton County GOP leaders Andrew Azan III and Tom Carroll spoke to Fox News Digital about their collective ground game.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Majority of Pennsylvania county sheriffs 'wholeheartedly endorse' Trump for president
item.description: EXCLUSIVE: Forty-nine of the 67 county sheriffs in battleground state Pennsylvania endorsed former President Trump on Thursday, saying they are “confident in his leadership and ability to safeguard our country.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: WATCH: Nevada US Senate Debate: Jacky Rosen and Sam Brown
item.description: As the seconds tick away until Election Day on Nov. 5, Democratic incumbent Sen. Jacky Rosen and Republican challenger Sam Brown are set to debate the issues Nevadans are most concerned about heading into 2025 and beyond.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Following death of Hamas leader, Harris says it's 'time for the day after to begin' in Gaza
item.description: Democratic presidential nominee Kamala Harris says the death of Hamas’ top leader, Yahya Sinwar, “gives us an opportunity to finally end the war in Gaza.”.
item.dc:creator: Chris Megerian And Colleen Long, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Oklahoma politicians debate ethics of 'paddling' students
item.description: There were 351 reported instances of Oklahoma students with disabilities who were stricken in schools during the 2020-21 school year.
item.dc:creator: Markie Martin, NewsNation

item.title: Mike Johnson kicks off swing-state tour as GOP clings to House control
item.description: House Speaker Mike Johnson is taking his "American Revival" tour to the swing states with less than three weeks until Election Day.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump delivers a pointed and at times bitter speech at Al Smith charity dinner
item.description: Former President Donald Trump laced into Vice President Kamala Harris and other Democrats in a pointed and at times bitter speech as he headlined the annual Al Smith charity dinner in New York.
item.dc:creator: Jill Colvin, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Biden admin issues warning to Netanyahu as Israel holds emergency meeting on Gaza aid
item.description: The U.S. warned Israel on this week that it is "watching" Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu's actions on delivering aid to Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Harris, Trump trade insults over who is fit to serve
item.description: The question of each presidential candidate's fitness to serve has become a breeding ground for insults between Vice President Harris and former President Trump, who are trying to paint each other as incapable of being the next commander in chief.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: The Latest: Harris keeps a focus on 'blue wall' states in the campaign's final weeks
item.description: With three weeks left in the presidential campaign, Democrat Kamala Harris is spending most of her days trying to shore up support in the “blue wall” states of Pennsylvania, Michigan and Wisconsin as she tries to avoid a repeat of Hillary Clinton’s collapse there eight years ago.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Biden administration forgives student loans for 60,000 public service workers
item.description: The Biden administration announced Thursday that it is forgiving the student loans for more than 60,000 public service workers. The move gives $4.5 billion in debt relief to teachers, firefighters and other eligible workers who have been paying on their loans for more than 10 years.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Trump tried a friendlier pitch with Latino voters at a Univision town hall
item.description: During a town hall outside Miami, Trump touted his record on the economy, yet called Jan. 6 a "day of love" and would not back off false claims about Haitian migrants eating pets in Springfield, Ohio.
item.dc:creator: Franco Ordoñez, MPR News

item.title: Trump spends big on anti-trans ads for 2024 election
item.description: Trump spends more on ads about transgender women and girls in sports and gender transitions than on Bidenomics, fracking and taxes.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Prann, NewsNation

item.title: Texas Supreme Court temporarily stops Robert Roberson’s execution
item.description: The state’s highest civil court said it wants a lower court to resolve a separation-of-powers issue raised by a group of Texas lawmakers who subpoenaed the death row inmate the night before he was set to be put to death.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Kayla Guo, KPRC 2

item.title: Canceling subscriptions has to be as easy as signing up, FTC says in a new rule
item.description: The "click-to-cancel" rule, now finalized by the Federal Trade Commission, aims to make it easier to cancel subscriptions and memberships. Companies argue the agency overstepped its authority.
item.dc:creator: Alina Selyukh, MPR News

item.title: Republicans grapple with storm of misinformation from their own party
item.description: Republicans representing states hit by hurricanes Helene and Milton are grappling with the spread of storm misinformation, much of which has stemmed from prominent figures in their own party — including sitting lawmakers and former President Trump.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Eric Adams welcomes new administration members amid wave of resignations
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Some new faces are joining Mayor Eric Adams' administration as it works to stabilize City Hall after a series of high-level exits. A new city schools chancellor and a new deputy mayor for public safety are taking the reins.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Control of the Minnesota House is up for grabs this fall. Here are 14 races to watch
item.description: While all seats in the Minnesota House of Representatives are on the ballot this fall, just a fraction are taking up the focus and much of the fundraising for the major parties.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Harris' Fox interview a bigger risk for Bret Baier: Bill O'Reilly
item.description: Bill O'Reilly discusses the state of the race as Kamala Harris and Donald Trump work to energize key voter groups.
item.dc:creator: Leland Vittert, NewsNation

item.title: Voting rights groups seek investigation into Wisconsin text message
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — Voting rights advocates on Tuesday asked state and federal authorities to investigate anonymous text messages apparently targeting young Wisconsin voters, warning them not to vote in a state where they are ineligible.
item.dc:creator: SCOTT BAUER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Nevada DMV fast-tracks credentials for potential voters
item.description: The Nevada Department of Motor Vehicles is seeking to move potential voters to the front of the line starting next week and is reminding new residents they can surrender their out-of-state ID.
item.dc:creator: James Schaeffer, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Election Day Looms: Hurricane impacts on voting
item.description: Voting rules changed for impacted Florida counties after hurricane Helene, now the question is, will rules be adjusted after hurricane Milton?
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Shannon Bilbray-Axelrod discusses growth, traffic, and affordable housing in county commissioner race
item.description: Shannon Bilbray-Axelrod, a candidate for county commissioner and current Nevada Assembly member, discussed her key priorities for her district on the 8 News Now Daily Voting Guide.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Election 2024: Races to watch in New Jersey
item.description: NEW JERSEY (PX11) -- It's been one of the most notable years for New Jersey politics in recent memory — indictments, upsets, and loss are just some themes of what has been a year that could shape the Garden State's political future for years to come.
item.dc:creator: Jim Vasil, PIX 11
